steps.title: Find angle A
steps.content:
The triangle is right, and angle $B$ is $30^\circ$. Since triangle angles sum to $180^\circ$,
$$
A+90^\circ+30^\circ=180^\circ
$$
$$
A=60^\circ
$$

steps.title: Use the special ratio
steps.content:
In a $30^\circ$-$60^\circ$-$90^\circ$ triangle, the sides opposite those angles are in the ratio
$$
1:\sqrt{3}:2
$$
For angle $A$, the opposite leg is 
$$
\sqrt{3}
$$ times the adjacent leg.

steps.title: Apply tangent
steps.content: Tangent is opposite over adjacent: $$ \tan A=\frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{adjacent}}=\sqrt{3} $$ The answer is C.
